mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) against rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward returns turn negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and corporate earnings pressure, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show positive mean performance following industrial production declines, or the signal exhibits zero predictive accuracy during demand-driven downturns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, higher borrowing costs compress corporate profit margins and increase discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally consistently during periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 3.0%, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a stable or falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that fuels wage inflation and equity valuation expansion over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence yields negative forward returns, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals strong economic momentum that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative following GDP accelerations, or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels correlate with increased corporate output and revenue generation, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical relationship with industrial production levels.
